body_txt:
Some people aren't very good at talking to or communicating with others. They could be painfully shy. They might even suffer from some past trauma or even a mental illness or affliction that hampers their ability to relate to or communicate with others. However, there might be situations where they will suddenly wax loquacious. This typically happens when they suddenly find themselves engaged in a topic about which they are both knowledgable and passionate, thus giving them the comfort and confidence to speak their mind. Indeed, during such moments of verbosity, they might not even realize themselves that they are talking to someone with whom they tend to have little in common, or with whom they normally wouldn't get along. In extreme cases, they may even veer into Sesquipedalian Loquaciousness . In some cases, they might present such confidence and knowledge on one particular topic that they might leave others Impressed by the Civilian . Their passion and knowledge for a particular subject could also make them seem Endearingly Dorky . Depending on their role, they could be a Socially Awkward Hero . Knowing that particular topics can get these individuals to be more talkative can be used by those who know them to get them to open up more freely, and possibly undergo a bit of Character Development . See also Shrinking Violet and its subtrope, Shy Blue-Haired Girl .
A subtrope of Situational Sociability .
